name: Ephesus
description: Ephesus, known in Turkish as Efes, holds a significant place in the cultural and historical landscape of Turkey, though its direct mythological presence within purely Turkish folklore is nuanced. While not explicitly detailed in traditional Turkish myths with gods and monsters, Ephesus’s historical and cultural importance has indirectly woven it into the broader tapestry of Turkish identity and storytelling. Its association with the Anatolian Mother Goddess, Cybele (later linked to Artemis/Diana), is relevant as Anatolian traditions predate and influenced Turkish culture. The Temple of Artemis, one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, stood as a symbol of this powerful female deity and the city's prosperity. With the advent of Christianity and its subsequent integration into Turkish society, Ephesus gained further significance as a site of early Christian history, particularly linked to the Virgin Mary and Saint Paul. The House of the Virgin Mary, near Ephesus, is revered by both Christians and Muslims in Turkey, embodying a syncretic spirituality that has been absorbed into Turkish tradition. The legends surrounding Ephesus, although not strictly 'Turkish mythology,' have contributed to the rich narrative heritage of the region, shaping perceptions of history, faith, and the enduring power of ancient sites. The city's ruins serve as a potent reminder of bygone eras, inspiring awe and fostering a sense of connection to the past, which is a crucial element of the Turkish cultural narrative. It represents a convergence of diverse cultural influences, from ancient Anatolian beliefs to Greek mythology, Roman civilization, and early Christianity, all contributing to the complex and layered historical identity of Turkey.
subtitle: Ancient city of gods and history

keyMyths.title: The Temple of Artemis
keyMyths.description: The Temple of Artemis at Ephesus was a colossal structure dedicated to the Greek goddess of the hunt, wilderness, wild animals, the Moon, and chastity. Its grandeur made it one of the Seven Wonders of the Ancient World, attracting pilgrims and worshippers from across the region.

keyMyths.title: The House of the Virgin Mary
keyMyths.description: Located near Ephesus, the House of the Virgin Mary is believed by many Christians and Muslims to be the place where Mary, the mother of Jesus, spent her last years. It is a site of pilgrimage and healing.

sources.author: Strabo
sources.description: Ancient Greek geographer and historian's account of Ephesus.
